methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: Total debts must exactly equal the sum of normalized total supply, unclaimed withdrawals, and accrued protocol fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If total debts are under-calculated, the market might allow borrowers to withdraw more collateral than they should, leading to insolvency and inability to pay lenders.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalDebts() public view { assert(totalDebts(state) == state.normalizeAmount(state.scaledTotalSupply) + state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals + state.accruedProtocolFees); }

invariants.description: The required liquidity must always be at least the sum of pending withdrawals, unclaimed withdrawals, and accrued protocol fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If liquidity required is less than the pending/unclaimed withdrawals, borrowers could drain funds that are actively being withdrawn by lenders, causing withdrawal failures.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_liquidityRequiredLowerBound() public view { assert(liquidityRequired(state) >= state.normalizeAmount(state.scaledPendingWithdrawals) + state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals + state.accruedProtocolFees); }

invariants.description: When a temporary reserve ratio is activated due to an APR reduction, the new temporary reserve ratio must be greater than or equal to the original reserve ratio.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the temporary reserve ratio could be lower, a borrower could exploit the APR reduction to simultaneously lower their reserve requirement, increasing risk to lenders while paying them less.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_temporaryReserveRatioMonotonicity() public view { assert(_calculateTemporaryReserveRatioBips(newApr, oldApr, oldReserve) >= oldReserve); }

invariants.description: Withdrawals cannot be queued before the fixed term end time.
invariants.attack_scenario: If lenders can queue withdrawals before the term ends, the fixed-term nature of the loan is broken, causing a liquidity crisis for the borrower who expected locked capital.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fixedTermWithdrawalLock() public view { if (block.timestamp < _hookedMarkets[market].fixedTermEndTime) { assert(withdrawal_reverts); } }

invariants.description: Borrowable assets must never exceed total assets minus unclaimed withdrawals and accrued fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If borrowable assets exceed this bound, borrowers can steal protocol fees or funds already claimed by lenders, leading to protocol insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_borrowableAssetsUpperBound() public view { assert(borrowableAssets(state, totalAssets) <= totalAssets - state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals - state.accruedProtocolFees); }

invariants.description: Withdrawable protocol fees must not exceed the total assets minus unclaimed withdrawals, nor the accrued protocol fees.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the protocol can withdraw more fees than accrued or dip into unclaimed withdrawals, it steals lender funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_withdrawableProtocolFeesBound() public view { assert(withdrawableProtocolFees(state, totalAssets) <= MathUtils.min(totalAssets - state.normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals, state.accruedProtocolFees)); }
